page: Amazon Web Services r8a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the hardware demonstrates top-tier performance across compute and memory subsystems. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core suites. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 33262.6 GB/sec and a large block uncached write bandwidth of 431.72 GB/sec. These capabilities translate to high-throughput operations, as evidenced by Redis SET performance reaching 451.09 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 170.4 GB/sec.

The instance is highly suited for memory-intensive and computationally demanding workloads. These include large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and large language model inference. For instance, prompt processing speeds reach 55.03 tokens/second for large 70B models, while text generation for medium 7B models operates at 46.23 tokens/second. The high density of dedicated physical cores and DDR5 memory provides a cost-efficient profile for consolidating enterprise databases and running memory-bound analytical pipelines without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at 4.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, with no local storage or GPUs. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmarks place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, alongside exceptional memory bandwidth. It is highly optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, software compilation, and large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for high-throughput database and memory-bound workloads.

tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise databases and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated physical AMD EPYC 9R45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET, and web serving
Optimized for large-scale databases, software compilation, and LLM inference
